publicSideBand: City-managed public-side work is covered under the Lafayette replacement program
privateSideBand: No direct charge to customers for identified private-side replacements in active project areas
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement is described as no direct charge to customers in the current Lafayette program phases
housingTypeAssumption: Lafayette publishes program-funded replacement language instead of contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on identification letters project-area scheduling and signed right-of-entry forms
restorationAssumption: Restoration and post-replacement testing are handled through the city-coordinated contractor process
methodologySummary: Lafayette ties its cost language to active project phases funded through grants and loans and to the city's posted 45-day construction notice and right-of-entry workflow rather than a citywide contractor quote.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when the address is in an active Lafayette project area and the owner returns the required right-of-entry materials so the city-coordinated contractor can complete replacement.
